---
title: "TERMINAL THEME"
subtitle: "A retro-terminal Quarto reveal.js theme"
format:
  terminal-revealjs:
    # terminal-hint defaults to "press → to continue" (set by the format);
    # override any terminal-* option here. Below we just set a default path.
    terminal-path: "demo/terminal-theme"
---

## SKILLS. {.section-break terminal-path="masterclass/module-04" terminal-status="LESSON 4 / 11"}

[// HERMES AGENT MASTERCLASS]{.kicker}

[Procedural memory you can author.]{.lede}

[80+ skills ship in the box. By the end of this module, you'll have written one.]{.desc}

## AN AI AGENT THAT LEARNS YOU. {terminal-path="part-01/overview" terminal-status="1.1"}

[Open source. Built by Nous Research. MIT licensed.]{.lede}

::: callouts

- Runs in your terminal (CLI) or on your phone (gateway)
- Works with any model — Claude, GPT, Grok, Kimi, local, whatever
- Nous uses this same tool to generate training data for the **Hermes model family**

:::

## THE BET: COMPOUNDING VALUE. {terminal-path="part-02/the-bet" terminal-status="2.1"}

Most agents are [stateless]{.neg} — every session starts from zero.
Hermes is **stateful by design**. Every session makes the next one better.

::: steps

1. You give it a task
2. It captures a **trajectory** — tools used, decisions made
3. It extracts a reusable **skill**
4. Next time: faster, cleaner, smarter

:::

## TWO FILES. {terminal-path="~/.hermes/memories/" terminal-status="CONFIG: memory:"}

::: cards

::: card
[MEMORY.MD]{.card-title}
Agent's notes about projects, environment, decisions, lessons learned.

cap **2,200 chars** · ~800 tokens
key: `memory_char_limit`
:::

::: card
[USER.MD]{.card-title}
Agent's model of who you are: role, preferences, communication style.

cap **1,375 chars** · ~500 tokens
key: `user_char_limit`
:::

:::

Plain text. **Editable. Auditable. Portable.**

## PART 03. {.section-break terminal-path="part-03" terminal-status="SECTION 3 / 5"}

[// FILE SYSTEM]{.kicker}

[Where everything lives.]{.lede}

[A section-break slide: vertically centered, oversized title. Use these as dividers between major parts of your talk.]{.desc}

## WHERE STUFF LIVES. {terminal-path="part-03/file-layout" terminal-status="3.3"}

[Everything Hermes stores is in ~/.hermes/]{.lede}

```text
~/.hermes/
├── config.yaml      # your settings — editable
├── .env             # your secrets — editable
├── memories/        # MEMORY.md + USER.md (Mod 5)
├── sessions/        # every past conversation
├── skills/          # installed skills (Mod 4)
├── logs/            # agent.log + errors.log
└── cron/            # scheduled jobs (Mod 8)
```

→ You can `cat` or edit the top 3. Don't touch `sessions/` or `cron/` by hand.
